At expert level, “Claude” means both the underlying large language models (LLMs) and the surrounding ecosystem: chat apps, coding agents, research tools, APIs, safety practices, and integrations that make those models usable in products and organizations.
What Claude is in formal terms
Claude is a proprietary family of large language models (LLMs) and AI assistants developed by Anthropic, a safety‑focused AI company founded in 2021 by former OpenAI researchers.
The Claude family powers an AI chatbot (
Claude.ai
and mobile apps), developer APIs, Claude Code (a coding agent), and other tools that can understand and generate text and code, reason about complex problems, and analyze images.
As of 2026, Claude models are considered “

Claude model families and variants
Anthropic releases Claude in generations (Claude 2, Claude 3, later 4.x) and in several size tiers per generation.
Claude 3 family
(historical but still important): Haiku, Sonnet, Opus.
Haiku: smallest, fastest, lowest cost, good for high‑volume, low‑latency tasks like customer chat.
Sonnet: mid‑tier, balances speed and intelligence, suitable for many production workloads.
Opus: largest, best reasoning/coding/analysis; more expensive and slower, used for the hardest problems.
Newer Claude 4.x variants (e.g., Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5) further improve coding,

multimodal
, meaning they can accept images as input and reason over charts, diagrams, screenshots, and other visual formats.

up to 1M tokens

attrs.definition: MMLU(Massive Multitask Language Understanding) is basically a big exam for AI models that checks how good they are at many school and university subjects. It is used to compare how smart different language models are at general knowledge and problem‑solving.

Layman’s explanation with real‑world examples
Claude in plain language
Think of Claude as a very smart digital colleague you can talk to in normal language.
You type questions or upload documents; Claude reads, thinks, and replies in clear English (or many other languages), explaining things, drafting text, or writing code for you.
Claude doesn’t just “autocomplete sentences”—it can follow complex instructions, break down problems, and ask clarifying questions, so it feels more like a thoughtful assistant than a simple chatbot.
